findings.evidence: `rg` finds the existing APIs in `Measure.lean`: `chebyshevTNormSq_zero` line 67, `chebyshevTNormSq_of_ne_zero` line 71, `normalizedChebyshevT_def` line 172, and `coeFn_normalizedChebyshevTLp` line 234; Mathlib also has `T_zero`/`T_one` in `Mathlib/RingTheory/Polynomial/Chebyshev.lean` lines 125 and 128.
findings.fix: Remove these exported lemmas and use `rw [normalizedChebyshevT_def, chebyshevTNormSq_zero]`, `rw [normalizedChebyshevT_def, chebyshevTNormSq_of_ne_zero hn]`, and `coeFn_normalizedChebyshevTLp` locally where needed.
findings.issue: `normalizedChebyshevT_zero_apply`, `normalizedChebyshevT_of_ne_zero_apply`, `coeFn_normalizedChebyshevTLp_zero`, and `coeFn_normalizedChebyshevTLp_of_ne_zero` are small rewrites of the existing general representative lemmas and norm constants, creating a parallel API.

summary: The new declarations are parallel wrapper lemmas over existing normalized Chebyshev and norm-square APIs rather than new reusable content.
